title: Ivory Coast defender breaks down in tears after prematurely leaving the 2026 World Cup.
original_text:
The image of the 25-year-old star breaking down in tears on the substitutes' bench left many fans heartbroken and became one of the most memorable moments of the second round of group stage matches at the 2026 World Cup.

In the match against Germany in Toronto (Canada), Wilfried Singo continued to demonstrate his crucial role in the Ivory Coast lineup. The Galatasaray player was energetic on the right flank, constantly contributing to both attack and defense. However, in the 82nd minute, the 2000-born defender suddenly clutched his left hamstring and signaled for a substitution after feeling unable to continue playing.

Immediately after leaving the pitch, Wilfried Singo sat on the bench with his shirt pulled down over his face. His tears spoke volumes about the disappointment and anxiety he was experiencing. His teammates and coaching staff constantly came to comfort and encourage the Ivorian star for the remainder of the match.

Currently, the medical team has not yet released an official diagnosis regarding the extent of Wilfried Singo's injury. However, initial assessments suggest that the player likely suffered a serious hamstring injury. With this type of injury, recovery time could range from several weeks to several months, depending on the severity of the damage. This means Wilfried Singo's chances of participating in the 2026 World Cup are almost nonexistent.

The 25-year-old defender's injury is all the more unfortunate given his impressive performance against Germany. For most of the match, Wilfried Singo was one of the standout players for the African side. Ivory Coast even pulled off a surprise by taking the lead thanks to Franck Kessie's goal in the 30th minute.

However, the game changed in the second half. Striker Deniz Undav scored in quick succession in the 68th and 90+4th minutes, helping Germany come from behind to win 2-1 and secure their place in the Round of 16 of the 2026 World Cup with two wins out of two matches.

Despite losing to Germany, Ivory Coast's chances of advancing in the 2026 World Cup remain wide open. Previously, the African representatives secured a crucial victory against Ecuador in their opening match, giving them a significant advantage in the race for a place in the knockout round. In their final Group E match on June 25th, Ivory Coast will face Curacao in a decisive game.

Source: https://danviet.vn/hau-ve-bo-bien-nga-bat-khoc-vi-som-phai-chia-tay-world-cup-2026-d1436936.
